This rodent is a Central American agouti (Dasyprocta punctata), a species common in tropical rainforests from Mexico to South America.
They are known for their vital ecological role as seed dispersers, often burying seeds to eat later and forgetting them, which helps grow new trees.
While similar in appearance to a large guinea pig or tailless squirrel, they have longer legs adapted for speed.
